description: Stable cross-session startup rule should promote from candidate to belief after repeated support.
target_claim: Prefer record-tagged profile hits first, then use reference docs only as fallback.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] bundle first
source_rows.claim: Load compact startup records before older operating manuals.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] compact startup stays small
source_rows.claim: Keep startup focused on core-policy, persona, soul, and project memory.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] startup falls back too early
source_rows.claim: Old reference docs should not outrank the compact profile bundle.
source_rows.trigger: Startup retrieval reaches for legacy docs before checking record-tagged bundle rows.
source_rows.symptom: The agent leans on old structure even when the new bundle already covers the decision.
source_rows.fix: Prefer record-tagged profile hits first, then use reference docs only as fallback.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] startup blob hides new layer
source_rows.claim: Legacy startup blobs should not displace compact record-tagged guidance.
source_rows.trigger: The system treats old operating docs as the first stop.
source_rows.symptom: Bundle-first startup never gets a clean chance to carry the decision.
source_rows.fix: Prefer record-tagged profile hits first, then use reference docs only as fallback.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] fallback stays secondary
source_rows.claim: Keep old startup references as fallback instead of the default operating payload.

description: A same-session burst should stay candidate-only and fail the distinct-session gate.
target_claim: Require repeated support across distinct sessions before promoting belief.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] burst compact 1
source_rows.claim: Keep startup rule #1 compact and inspectable.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] burst compact 2
source_rows.claim: Keep startup rule #2 compact and inspectable.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] burst compact 3
source_rows.claim: Keep startup rule #3 compact and inspectable.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] burst compact 4
source_rows.claim: Keep startup rule #4 compact and inspectable.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] burst follow-up
source_rows.claim: A single session burst should not become a durable belief.
source_rows.trigger: Many similar observations arrive in one short burst.
source_rows.fix: Require repeated support across distinct sessions before promoting belief.

description: Contradictory runtime advice should remain candidate-only and be blocked by contradiction.
target_claim: Move back to one canonical runtime path after the cutover window closes.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] canonical path
source_rows.claim: Keep one canonical runtime path for bridge state.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] temporary window
source_rows.claim: However, keep a temporary fallback path during cutover windows.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] split path drift
source_rows.claim: Split runtime paths break trust in recall.
source_rows.trigger: Interactive and automation paths write to different stores.
source_rows.fix: Move back to one canonical runtime path after the cutover window closes.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] runtime trust
source_rows.claim: Trust in recall depends on a shared runtime path.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] post-cutover cleanup
source_rows.claim: Re-unify runtime paths after temporary cutover windows close.

description: A once-strong candidate that goes stale should be visible as stale rather than promoted.
target_claim: Prefer bundle-first retrieval, then use legacy docs only as fallback.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] bundle first retrieval
source_rows.claim: Load bundle-first retrieval before older startup manuals.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] compact startup wins
source_rows.claim: Keep compact startup guidance ahead of legacy reference startup.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] legacy docs outrank bundle
source_rows.claim: Legacy startup docs should not outrank the compact profile bundle.
source_rows.trigger: Startup retrieval reaches for old manuals before record-tagged rows.
source_rows.symptom: The agent keeps falling back to legacy startup material.
source_rows.fix: Prefer bundle-first retrieval, then use legacy docs only as fallback.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] startup blob hides bundle
source_rows.claim: Legacy startup blobs should not displace compact record-tagged guidance.
source_rows.trigger: The system treats old operating docs as the first stop.
source_rows.symptom: Bundle-first startup never gets a clean chance to carry the decision.
source_rows.fix: Prefer bundle-first retrieval, then use legacy docs only as fallback.

description: If the compressed claim shifts across runs, the ladder should block belief on stability grounds.
target_claim: Use executable proof as the first validation boundary.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] verify before done
source_rows.claim: Verify behavior before calling work done.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] skipped verification
source_rows.claim: Skipping verification weakens trust in memory.
source_rows.trigger: The agent marks work complete before checking behavior.
source_rows.fix: Verify the actual behavior before calling work done.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] preserve proof
source_rows.claim: Keep executable proof close to the claim.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] trust through verification
source_rows.claim: Trust grows when behavior is verified instead of guessed.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] changed rule
source_rows.claim: Proof-first discipline beats intuition-only review.
source_rows.trigger: Review focuses on prose instead of behavior.
source_rows.symptom: A different operating rule wins the second cycle.
source_rows.fix: Use executable proof as the first validation boundary.

description: A real pattern with early support should stay candidate-only until the stronger gate is met.
target_claim: Store machine-readable, token-efficient records instead of narrative memory.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] machine readable records
source_rows.claim: Store machine-readable, token-efficient records instead of narrative memory.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] summaries are source material
source_rows.claim: Summaries should be source material rather than the final durable record.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] chat-shaped memory
source_rows.claim: Chat-shaped memory becomes expensive and hard to reuse.
source_rows.trigger: A summary keeps transcript wording instead of structured claims.
source_rows.fix: Store machine-readable, token-efficient records instead of narrative memory.

description: Cross-domain spillover guidance with repeated caveats should remain candidate-only and surface contradiction pressure.
target_claim: Focused replay filtered to domain:retrieval still synthesizes adjacent domains early, especially domain:memory-bridge and then domain:reliability. That spillover is real on live-ish data and should be treated as part of domain-synthesis diagnostics, not as a harness bug.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] replay slice spillover
source_rows.claim: Focused replay should report out-of-filter domains alongside candidate totals.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] replay spillover caveat
source_rows.claim: However, a retrieval-focused replay can synthesize adjacent domains before it settles.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] replay spillover is real
source_rows.claim: Focused retrieval replay spills into adjacent domains on live-ish data.
source_rows.trigger: A domain-filtered replay still emits domain-note activity outside the target slice.
source_rows.symptom: Reviewers assume the harness is broken instead of reading the synthesis boundary.
source_rows.fix: Focused replay filtered to domain:retrieval still synthesizes adjacent domains early, especially domain:memory-bridge and then domain:reliability. That spillover is real on live-ish data and should be treated as part of domain-synthesis diagnostics, not as a harness bug.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] replay spillover is signal
source_rows.claim: But cross-domain spillover is diagnostic signal rather than a harness failure.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] replay slice exceptions
source_rows.claim: Except for very narrow slices, replay often spills into memory-bridge before reliability.

description: Candidate aging guidance with several qualified claims should stay blocked by contradiction even with cross-session support.
target_claim: Belief replay is more useful when it can age belief-candidate rows between windows. That lets us verify that stale candidate history does not count toward stability or promotion, instead of assuming freshness only from reviewed fixtures.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] replay aging hook
source_rows.claim: Belief replay should support aging candidate rows between windows.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] replay aging caution
source_rows.claim: However, age candidate rows only after a clear replay boundary so synthetic freshness does not leak across windows.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] stale candidate history
source_rows.claim: Fresh-looking candidate history can hide stale support unless replay can age candidates between windows.
source_rows.trigger: A replay keeps prior candidate timestamps fresh forever.
source_rows.symptom: Belief promotion looks stronger than the underlying evidence really is.
source_rows.fix: Belief replay is more useful when it can age belief-candidate rows between windows. That lets us verify that stale candidate history does not count toward stability or promotion, instead of assuming freshness only from reviewed fixtures.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] replay aging qualifier
source_rows.claim: But stale candidate history should only count when the replay intentionally simulates time passing.

description: The policy-boundary bridge case should remain candidate-only when support rows mix bridge language with explicit manual-policy caveats.
target_claim: A conservative internal belief_candidate layer should sit between compressed domain patterns and core policy. It should require stronger support than ordinary domain-note synthesis, track contradiction_count and evidence_refs, and remain revisable instead of auto-promoting itself into policy.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] belief bridge
source_rows.claim: A belief_candidate layer should bridge domain-note compression and manual policy.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] policy stays manual
source_rows.claim: However, core policy should still stay manual while the new ladder is proving itself.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] bridge is not policy
source_rows.claim: A belief bridge becomes risky when the system treats it like auto-policy.
source_rows.trigger: The ladder jumps from compressed domain patterns straight into policy language.
source_rows.symptom: The system overclaims a belief candidate as if it were already a stable core rule.
source_rows.fix: A conservative internal belief_candidate layer should sit between compressed domain patterns and core policy. It should require stronger support than ordinary domain-note synthesis, track contradiction_count and evidence_refs, and remain revisable instead of auto-promoting itself into policy.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] bridge caveat
source_rows.claim: But belief_candidate remains a bridge layer, not a license to automate policy writing.

description: Ordinary fallback-vs-default boundary wording should not be treated as contradiction when the overall rule still promotes bundle-first startup.
target_claim: Prefer record-tagged profile hits first, then use reference docs only as fallback.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] bundle-first startup stays primary
source_rows.claim: Load compact startup records before older operating manuals.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] fallback boundary wording
source_rows.claim: But keep old startup references as fallback instead of the default operating payload.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] legacy docs outrank bundle again
source_rows.claim: Old reference docs should not outrank the compact profile bundle.
source_rows.trigger: Startup retrieval reaches for legacy docs before checking record-tagged bundle rows.
source_rows.symptom: The agent leans on old structure even when the new bundle already covers the decision.
source_rows.fix: Prefer record-tagged profile hits first, then use reference docs only as fallback.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] bundle-first should lead
source_rows.claim: Legacy startup blobs should not displace compact record-tagged guidance.
source_rows.trigger: The system treats old operating docs as the first stop.
source_rows.symptom: Bundle-first startup never gets a clean chance to carry the decision.
source_rows.fix: Prefer record-tagged profile hits first, then use reference docs only as fallback.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] fallback stays secondary
source_rows.claim: Keep old startup references as fallback rather than the default operating payload.

description: Manual-policy boundary wording should stay compatible with belief promotion when it only narrows where policy changes happen, rather than disputing the bridge rule itself.
target_claim: Use belief_candidate as the bridge between compressed domain patterns and manual core-policy review.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] belief bridge stays explicit
source_rows.claim: Use belief_candidate as the bridge between compressed domain patterns and manual core-policy review.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] policy boundary stays manual
source_rows.claim: However, keep core policy manual while belief evidence is still accumulating.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] bridge before policy rewrite
source_rows.claim: Skipping the belief bridge makes policy updates feel premature.
source_rows.trigger: Compressed patterns are treated like final policy before enough evidence accumulates.
source_rows.symptom: Core policy review gets noisy because every pattern looks policy-ready.
source_rows.fix: Use belief_candidate as the bridge between compressed domain patterns and manual core-policy review.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] bridge narrows policy changes
source_rows.claim: Policy changes become cleaner when belief_candidate absorbs pattern churn first.
source_rows.trigger: Every compressed pattern competes to rewrite policy directly.
source_rows.symptom: Manual policy review is flooded with unstable proposals.
source_rows.fix: Use belief_candidate as the bridge between compressed domain patterns and manual core-policy review.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] manual review remains downstream
source_rows.claim: Keep core policy review manual even after a belief has stabilized.

description: Project-vs-global placement wording should stay compatible with belief promotion when it only narrows memory placement scope instead of disputing the governance rule.
target_claim: Write project-specific durable lessons to project memory, and keep global core for durable operating lessons.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] project lessons stay local
source_rows.claim: Write project-specific durable lessons to project memory.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] global core stays narrow
source_rows.claim: However, keep project-specific workflow rules in project memory while the global core stays reserved for durable operating lessons.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] project drift in global core
source_rows.claim: Writing repo-specific workflow rules into the global core creates drift.
source_rows.trigger: A project lesson is stored into a cross-project operating namespace.
source_rows.symptom: The global core starts carrying workflow details that belong to one repository.
source_rows.fix: Write project-specific durable lessons to project memory, and keep global core for durable operating lessons.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] global core gets noisy
source_rows.claim: The global core gets noisy when project-specific workflow rules accumulate there.
source_rows.trigger: Cross-project memory is used as the default landing zone for project-specific lessons.
source_rows.symptom: Startup carries local workflow baggage that does not belong in every session.
source_rows.fix: Write project-specific durable lessons to project memory, and keep global core for durable operating lessons.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] writeback keeps scope clean
source_rows.claim: Keep project-specific workflow rules in project memory instead of teaching them to the global core.

description: Startup-eligibility narrowing is worth watching, but for now it should stay blocked as contradiction until broader dogfood proves it is only a safe boundary and not a real counter-claim.
target_claim: Keep transparency and resource-stewardship parked as a candidate until startup eligibility is proven by broader dogfood.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] startup bundle already suppresses recklessness
source_rows.claim: The current startup bundle already suppresses reckless automation through manual-baseline and reversibility policies.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] startup eligibility narrowing
source_rows.claim: But transparency and resource-stewardship still read more like orchestration guidance than universal profile identity.

source_rows.title: [[Gotcha]] eager startup promotion
source_rows.claim: Promoting startup-eligibility candidates too early can overload the bundle with guidance that has not proved itself yet.
source_rows.trigger: A new guidance pattern is treated as startup-default before broader dogfood.
source_rows.symptom: Startup carries more policy-like text than the current evidence supports.
source_rows.fix: Keep transparency and resource-stewardship parked as a candidate until startup eligibility is proven by broader dogfood.

source_rows.title: [[Learn]] startup eligibility remains provisional
source_rows.claim: Keep startup eligibility narrow while dogfood still decides what belongs in the eager bundle.
